name: Ravenna CCS Phase 1 (Eni/Snam, Adriatic)
source: Eni/Snam press release Sep 2024; Offshore Magazine Oct 2024 (offshore CO2 injection underway); CCSA case study 2024; OnePetro OMC-2025-675 (first 8 months of operations)
notes: Italy's first CCS project; Phase 1 operational since Aug 2024. CO2 captured from Eni gas treatment plant at Casalborsetti (Ravenna municipality), transported offshore ~20 km and injected into Porto Corsini Mare Ovest depleted gas reservoir at ~3,000 m depth, Adriatic Sea. Phase 1 capacity 0.025 Mtpa (25,000 tpa); capture efficiency >90%. Phase 2 targets ~4 Mtpa by 2030, potential expansion to 16 Mtpa as Mediterranean decarbonisation hub. Coordinates reflect offshore injection point (offshore from lat 44.51°N, 12.23°E Casalborsetti).

name: Northern Endurance Partnership / Endurance (East Coast Cluster, UK)
source: NEP press release Dec 2024 (FID / financial close); Offshore Magazine Dec 2024; UK government CCS announcement Jun 2025 (£9.4B capital budget); East Coast Cluster website
notes: UK's first CCS project to achieve financial close (Dec 2024). CO2 collected from Teesside and Humber industrial emitters (East Coast Cluster) and stored in Endurance saline aquifer ~75 km east of Flamborough Head, ~1,000 m below North Sea seabed. Phase 1 capacity 4 Mtpa; long-term ambition up to 27 Mtpa by mid-2030s. Transport and storage infrastructure developed by bp, Equinor, TotalEnergies (NEP). Construction from mid-2025; start-up 2028. Coordinates approximate for Endurance storage site.

name: Liverpool Bay CCS / HyNet T&S (Eni, Irish Sea)
source: Eni FID press release Apr 2025; SPE JPT Apr 2025; Liverpool Bay T&S website liverpoolbay-ts.co.uk; UK government Jun 2025 (£21.7B over 25 years for Track-1 clusters)
notes: FID reached Apr 2025. Eni operator. Stores CO2 in depleted Hamilton, Hamilton North, and Lennox offshore gas fields (Irish Sea, west of Wirral Peninsula). Phase 1 capacity 4.5 Mtpa; expandable to 10 Mtpa by 2030s. Serves HyNet North West industrial cluster. Construction 2025; start-up 2028. Coordinates approximate for Hamilton field area.

name: Viking CCS (Harbour Energy / bp, Southern North Sea)
source: Harbour Energy Viking CCS website; Energy Voice 2024; New Civil Engineer Apr 2025 (DCO granted); Global CCS Institute Status Report 2024; Viking CCS independently verified 300 Mt storage capacity
notes: UK Track-2 project. Development consent order (DCO) granted Apr 2025. Depleted Viking gas fields ~140 km offshore in Southern North Sea, 2.7 km below seabed. Independently verified 300 Mt CO2 storage capacity. Target 10 Mtpa by 2030. Partners: Harbour Energy (operator), bp, RWE, Phillips 66, VPI. Start-up target 2027+ pending FID. Ship-based CO2 transport agreement signed with Cory/ABP.

name: Bifrost CCS Phase 1 (TotalEnergies/DUC, Harald West & Dagny, Denmark)
source: Bifrost CCS website bifrost-ccs.com; TotalEnergies Bifrost press releases 2024-2025; EUDP funding announcement; Enerdata Dec 2024; EU PCI designation
notes: EU Project of Common Interest. Distinct from existing 'Greensand CCS (Denmark)' site (which uses Nini West). Bifrost Phase 1 uses Harald West depleted gas field and Dagny saline structure ~250 km offshore west Denmark. Phase 1 target ≥5 Mtpa from ~2030; total potential 10-15 Mtpa; estimated 335 Mt total storage. Consortium: TotalEnergies (coordinator), BlueNord, Nordsøfonden, Ørsted, DTU. Coordinates approximate for Harald West / Dagny license area.

name: Petrobras Santos Basin Pre-Salt CO2 Reinjection (Brazil)
source: Offshore Energy 2025 (22 FPSOs reinjection record); Rio Times Online 2024 (28% global CCS); ScienceDirect Springer J Pet Explor Prod Tech 2021 (doi 10.1007/s13202-021-01102-8); MIT CCS project: Lula
notes: World's largest CO2 injection program by annual volume. 22 FPSOs in Santos Basin pre-salt (SE Brazil) equipped with CO2 separation and reinjection systems (produced gas contains 8-15% CO2). Record 14.2 Mt CO2 reinjected in 2024 alone (est. ~28% of world CCS capacity that year). Static storage capacity estimated >3.3 Gt in four primary fields (Lula, Sapinhoa, Buzios, Tupi). Primarily EOR/production-management rather than dedicated CCS, but substantial permanent storage occurs. Centroid coordinate approximate for Santos Basin pre-salt polygon offshore Rio de Janeiro state.

name: CNPC Jilin CO2-EOR CCS (Songyuan, China)
source: Global CCS Institute 2018 press release (world's 18th large-scale facility); CNPC 2018 CCS-EOR presentation; MIT CCS project: Jilin; CO2 EOR Jilin ScienceDirect 2014 (doi 10.1016/j.petrol.2014.12.009)
notes: China's first commercial CO2-EOR CCS project. CO2 sourced from Changling natural gas processing plant (~22.5% CO2 in gas), transported by pipeline to Jilin oilfield for EOR. Operational as pilot 2008; commercial scale (0.6 Mtpa) from 2018. Cumulative injection >1.1 Mt. Jilin oilfield located in Songyuan district, Jilin Province, NE China. Coordinates approximate for Jilin oilfield area.

name: CNOOC Enping 15-1 Offshore CCS (Pearl River Mouth Basin, China)
source: CNOOC commissioning Jun 2023; Offshore Magazine 2023; CGTN Sep 2025 (>100M m3 stored); The Chemical Engineer 2023; South China Morning Post 2023
notes: China's first offshore CCS project. Located in Pearl River Mouth Basin, South China Sea, ~190 km SW of Hong Kong, water depth ~80 m. CO2 separated from Enping 15-1 oilfield production gas and injected into saline layer ~800 m below seabed. Peak capacity ~0.3 Mtpa; cumulative storage >100 million m3 as of Sep 2025. Largest offshore oil production platform in Asia. Coordinates approximate for Enping 15-1 block (GEM Wiki: lat ~21.5, lon ~115.1).

name: Guohua Jinjie CCS (China Energy/CHN, Shaanxi)
source: Global CCS Institute Jan 2021; China Energy Investment Corp announcement Jan 2021; ScienceDirect (doi 10.1016/j.seppur.2023.124867); Global Energy Observatory (lat 38.7363, lon 110.1668)
notes: China's largest post-combustion CCS demonstration project at coal-fired power plant. Operational since Jan 2021. Located at Guohua Jinjie power station, Shenmu, Yulin, Shaanxi. Captures 150,000 tpa CO2 from coal power flue gas at >90% capture rate, injected into nearby oilfield for EOR. Coordinates from Global Energy Observatory record for Jinjie Coal Power Plant.

name: China Resources Haifeng CCS (Shanwei, Guangdong)
source: GEM Wiki: Shanwei Haifeng CCS power station; SCCS project info 1301 (Haifeng Power Plant CCS Plan); Guangdong Province CCUS demonstration listing 2023; ADB CCS Roadmap PRC 2022
notes: China Resources Power Haifeng coal-fired power plant CCS (4,100 MW+ station). Small pilot (20,000 tpa capture) operational since ~2019. Full-scale 1 Mtpa CCS expansion designated as Guangdong Province CCUS demonstration unit; reported in 'rapid construction' Sep 2023. CO2 to be injected into deep saline formation in South China Sea (~90-150 km offshore) or CNOOC EOR sites. Location: Xiaomo Town Da'ao Village, Haifeng, Shanwei.

name: Saudi Aramco Uthmaniyah CO2-EOR (Ghawar, Saudi Arabia)
source: MIT CCS project: Uthmaniyah; CSLF project profile; Aramco/KAPSARC announcement Jul 2015; OGCI CCUS Saudi Arabia report 2021; Aramco Europe CCUS page
notes: Saudi Arabia's first CCS project, operational Jul 2015. Captures 0.8 Mtpa CO2 from Hawiyah NGL Recovery Plant; compressed, dehydrated, transported ~85 km pipeline to Uthmaniyah sector of Ghawar oil field for CO2-EOR in Jurassic carbonate limestone. First large-scale CCS-EOR project in Middle East. Coordinates approximate for Uthmaniyah sector of Ghawar (~24.65°N, 49.15°E).

name: ADNOC Habshan CCUS (Al Gharbia, UAE)
source: ADNOC FID/investment announcement 2023; ADNOC Gas $615M contract award 2023; Shell Cansolv technology press release 2023; AGBI Nov 2024 (go-ahead); JPT 2023
notes: One of MENA's largest integrated CCS projects. FID by ADNOC Gas 2023. Captures 1.5 Mtpa CO2 at Habshan gas processing plant (Al Gharbia, Abu Dhabi), injects into Bab Far North geological formations ~150 miles SW. Uses Shell Cansolv absorption technology. Commissioning expected 2026. Distinct from existing Al Reyadah CCS (steel sector, 0.8 Mtpa). Coordinates: Habshan gas complex at ~23°49'N, 53°37'E (Wikipedia/Habshan article).

name: QatarEnergy Ras Laffan CCS
source: SCCS Edinburgh project info 2724; Carbon Herald 2024; QatarEnergy CCS press releases; Blackridge Research 2024; Anadolu Agency (5M tpa by 2025 target)
notes: Operational since ~2019 (earlier smaller-scale phases from 2012). Captures CO2 from LNG and gas processing at Ras Laffan Industrial City (~80 km N of Doha); injects into Arab Formation geological storage. Current active capacity 2.2 Mtpa; cumulative ~7.5 Mt stored to date. Expansion plans: 7-9 Mtpa by 2030, >11 Mtpa by 2035. World's largest CCS in LNG sector. New 4.1 Mtpa plant under construction. Coordinates: Ras Laffan Industrial City (25.93°N, 51.61°E).

name: Petronas Kasawari CCS (Block SK316, Sarawak, Malaysia)
source: NSEnergy Business Kasawari CCS project profile; Petronas FID Nov 2022; The Edge Malaysia 2025 (EPCIC contract RM4.5B to MMHE Aug 2025); Offshore Energy 2022; GEM Wiki Kasawari field (lat 4.981731, lon 112.972289)
notes: Positioned as world's largest offshore CCS project by volume. FID Nov 2022. Located in Block SK316, Central Luconia Province, ~200 km offshore Bintulu, Sarawak, ~108 m water depth. High-CO2 gas field; CO2 captured offshore and reinjected into M1 depleted field at 3.3 Mtpa. Start-up targeted end-2025/2026 (some sources cite 2027 push). First CCS project in Malaysia. Coordinates from GEM Wiki.

name: BP Tangguh CCUS / UCC (Bintuni Bay, West Papua, Indonesia)
source: BP FID press release Nov 2024; Offshore Energy Nov 2024 ($7B project); BP Indonesia Tangguh EGR/CCUS webpage; Global Energy Observatory (lat -2.44314, lon 133.139)
notes: FID November 2024 as part of $7B Tangguh Upstream Compression and CCS (UCC) project. Indonesia's most advanced CCUS project. Bintuni Bay, West Papua. Initial phase targets ~15 Mt total CO2 sequestration from Tangguh LNG operations; ultimate reservoir storage capacity ~1.8 Gt. CO2 injected back into gas reservoir for EGR + permanent storage. Production / injection start ~2028. Coordinates from Global Energy Observatory for Tangguh LNG plant.

name: Project Tundra (Minnkota Power, North Dakota, USA)
source: NDIC NDIC approvals announcement; DOE FONSI EA-2197 Sep 2024; Grand Forks Herald 2023 (up to $350M DOE grant); Project Tundra website projecttundrand.com; GetAMap (lat 47.0658, lon -101.2131)
notes: Would be world's largest post-combustion CO2 capture and geologic storage project. Captures up to 90% of CO2 from Minnkota Power's Milton R. Young lignite-fired station (Oliver County, ND) = ~4 Mtpa. Injects into Broom Creek (~1 mile deep) and Deadwood formations (~2 miles). Class VI permits approved by NDIC 2022. Up to $350M DOE grant (Dec 2023). FID pending federal/state economic framework. Coordinates from GetAMap for Milton R. Young Station.

name: Red Trail Energy CCS / Gevo North Dakota (Richardton, ND, USA)
source: Ethanol Producer Magazine Jun 2022; EcoEngineers project spotlight; Puro.earth 2024 CORC audit; Gevo/Red Trail acquisition press release Sep 2024; Decarb Connect project profile
notes: First US project to generate Puro.earth CO2 Removal Certificates (CORCs) for ethanol fermentation CCS (Mar 2024). Operational since Jun 2022. 64 MGY corn ethanol plant near Richardton, ND. Captures ~100% of fermentation CO2 (~180,000 tpa); injects via Class VI well into Broom Creek Formation ~6,500 ft below surface. Acquired by Gevo Inc. for $210M in Sep 2024. Coordinates approximate for Richardton, ND (46.88°N, 102.31°W).

name: Core Energy CO2-EOR (Northern Niagaran Reef Trend, Michigan, USA)
source: MIT CCS Otsego Northern Reef Trend fact sheet; NETL MRCSP Phase II Michigan Basin; EPA MRV reports Core Energy 2018-2021; MRCSP coordinates (45.042960, -84.482461)
notes: Operational since ~2003. CO2-EOR in Niagaran Pinnacle Reef Trend, Michigan Basin (~6,000 ft depth). CO2 sourced from DTE Energy Antrim Shale gas processing plant (~1,000 tpa/day = ~0.18 Mtpa). Cumulative injection hundreds of thousands of tonnes. One of the longest-running CO2-EOR with permanent storage monitoring in the US. Coordinates from NETL MRCSP Phase II (Otsego County, State-Charlton 30/31 field).

name: Stratos DAC + CCS (1PointFive / Occidental, Ector County, Texas, USA)
source: 1PointFive Ector County project page; Oxy/EPA Class VI permits approval Apr 2025; OGJ Apr 2025; RBN Energy 2025 (Q2 2026 startup target); Oxy news release
notes: World's largest Direct Air Capture facility. Located near Odessa/Penwell, Ector County, west Texas. Captures up to 0.5 Mtpa CO2 from atmosphere. Three Class VI injection wells (BRP CCS1-3) permitted Apr 2025; first-ever Class VI permits for DAC-sourced CO2. Inject into Permian Lower Sand Andres Formation, ~4,500-5,100 ft depth, near Penwell. Phase 1 startup target Q2 2026. Two capture trains in wet commissioning as of 2025. Coordinates approximate for Ector County/Odessa area.

name: Shell Polaris CCS / Atlas Carbon Storage Hub (Scotford, Alberta, Canada)
source: Shell FID press release Jun 2024; Alberta Major Projects (Polaris and Atlas entries); Canada.ca deputy PM visit Aug 2024; World Oil Jun 2024; Shell Scotford GEO coordinates (53.80137, -113.08992)
notes: FID Jun 2024. Shell Polaris captures ~650,000 tpa CO2 from Scotford refinery and chemicals complex (~40 km NE of Edmonton, AB). CO2 transported via 22 km pipeline to ATCO EnPower's Atlas Carbon Storage Hub, stored >2 km underground in Basal Cambrian Sands. Atlas Hub Phase 1: capacity 0.65 Mtpa; lifetime storage potential ~300 Mt. Operations expected late 2028. Creates ~2,000 jobs. Coordinates from Global Energy Observatory for Shell Scotford complex.

name: Pathways Alliance (Oil Sands Alliance) CCS Hub (Cold Lake, Alberta, Canada)
source: Oil Sands Alliance project overview (rebrand Feb 2026); Global CCS Institute Pathways profile Oct 2025; Alberta Energy Regulator application Mar 2024; Pathways Alliance fact sheet Jun 2025
notes: Canada's largest proposed CCS project ($16.5B). 400 km CO2 pipeline from >20 oil sands facilities in NE Alberta (Fort McMurray/Cold Lake region) to underground storage hub near Cold Lake. CO2 injected into Basal Cambrian Sandstone 1,000-2,000 m depth. Target: 22 Mtpa CO2 reduction by 2030. AER regulatory application filed Mar 2024. FID contingent on federal/provincial economic framework. Partners: Canadian Natural Resources, Cenovus, ConocoPhillips, Imperial Oil, MEG Energy, Suncor (>95% oil sands production). Coordinates approximate for Cold Lake storage hub.

name: Enbridge Open Access Wabamun Carbon Hub (Alberta, Canada)
source: Enbridge press releases 2022-2025; Alberta Major Projects: Open Access Wabamun Carbon Hub; Enbridge milestones page Oct 2025; Alberta Innovates support spring 2024
notes: Enbridge awarded evaluation rights 2022 (~220,000 ha west/north of Edmonton). Two appraisal wells drilled 2023-2024. Sequestration agreement signed Oct 2025 for North Area Basal Sandstone Unit. Open-access utility-scale CO2 storage. Currently contracted: Heidelberg Materials Edmonton Capture Project (~1.3 Mtpa). Also serves Air Products Canada Net-zero Hydrogen Energy Complex. FID not yet reached. Coordinates approximate for Wabamun Lake area (~53.5°N, 114.5°W).

name: ExxonMobil Baytown Low-Carbon Hydrogen + CCS (Texas, USA)
source: ExxonMobil Baytown hydrogen viewpoint (corporate.exxonmobil.com); CCUS Expo 2024; Calpine/ExxonMobil CO2 T&S agreement Apr 2025; ExxonMobil CCS business (16 Mtpa under contract, 2025)
notes: Proposed $7B low-carbon hydrogen plant at Baytown complex, TX. Would produce 1 Bcf/d low-carbon hydrogen and capture 7.5 Mtpa CO2 for permanent storage. FID expected 2024 but facing delays/potential cancellation following May 2025 DOE CCS grant cancellations ($3.7B revoked). Part of ExxonMobil TX/LA/MS CCS pipeline network (Denbury acquisition 2023; ~16 Mtpa under CCS contract as of Apr 2025). Status: planned/at-risk. Coordinates approximate for Baytown refining complex (~29.74°N, 95.0°W).

name: CO2CRC Otway International Test Centre (Victoria, Australia)
source: Global Energy Observatory record 40159 (lat -38.553394, lon 142.89484); CO2CRC Otway Project website; CSIRO Otway page; CSLF Stage 2 project profile
notes: Australia's key CCS research and demonstration site. Designated Australian National Research Asset. Near Port Campbell, SW Victoria (~300 km from Melbourne). CO2 injected into depleted Waarre C natural gas reservoir ~2 km depth since 2008. Over 65,000 t stored in initial phase; subsequent monitoring and injection phases ongoing. Not commercial scale; capacity estimate reflects ongoing research injection rate (~50 ktpa). Coordinates from Global Energy Observatory.

name: Bayu-Undan CCS (Santos, Timor Sea, Timor-Leste)
source: Santos press releases 2022-2024; Offshore Magazine 2022 (FEED initiated); IEEFA Bayu-Undan CCS analysis 2024; ICN Gateway project 4891; Bayu-Undan field: ~500 km from Darwin in Timor Sea
notes: Santos plans to repurpose depleted Bayu-Undan gas/condensate field (Timor Sea, ~500 km from Darwin, ~250 km S of Timor-Leste) as regional CO2 storage hub. Potential 10 Mtpa capacity (~1 Mtpa initial); MoUs signed for CO2 supply. FEED nearly complete. Target ready 2028 (Santos purchasing interim carbon credits). Positioned as regional storage hub for Asian industrial emitters. IEEFA (2024) flagged commercial/financing challenges. Timor-Leste JV partner: TIMOR GAP. Coordinates approximate for Bayu-Undan field.

name: Northern Lights CCS Phase 2 Expansion (Norway)
source: TotalEnergies Phase 2 announcement Mar 2025; Northern Lights website; Equinor Smeaheia page
notes: Capacity increment above Phase 1 (already in existing dataset as 'Northern Lights CCS Phase 1' at 1.5 Mtpa). Phase 2 announced Mar 2025 increases total injection from 1.5 to minimum 5.0 Mtpa (+3.5 Mtpa net new). First CO2 received Aug 2025. Storage same location ~2,500 m below North Sea seabed south of Troll field. Owned equally by TotalEnergies, Equinor, Shell. Coordinate same as Phase 1 entry.

name: Equinor Smeaheia CO2 Storage (North Sea, Norway)
source: Equinor Smeaheia project page; OGJ Apr 2022 (license awarded); Offshore Magazine 2024 (two appraisal wells drilled late 2024); Equinor award press release Apr 2022
notes: License awarded to Equinor (100% WI) Apr 2022. Large saline aquifer east of Troll field, offshore west Norway. Two appraisal wells completed late 2024. Ambition: up to 20 Mtpa CO2 from 2030s; potential to serve major European industrial clusters. Target reservoirs in Jurassic Viking and Dunlin Groups. CO2 pipeline to connect to Smeaheia and adjacent stores (Kinno, Abondigas). First injection expected early 2030s. Distinct from existing Sleipner/Snøhvit/Northern Lights sites.

name: Offshore Western Kyushu CCS (ENEOS / JX Nippon / J-POWER, Japan)
source: JOGMEC Advanced CCS Projects selection FY2024; ENEOS/JX Nippon/J-POWER/West Japan Carbon press release Oct 2024; Enerdata 2024; TankTerminals.com 2024; METI CCS commercialisation report Jun 2024
notes: One of nine JOGMEC-selected Advanced CCS Projects (Japan, FY2024). Consortium: ENEOS, JX Nippon Oil & Gas Exploration, J-POWER, West Japan Carbon. Engineering design and exploratory drilling phase post-FY2023 feasibility. 1.7 Mtpa CO2 from refineries and thermal power plants in Setouchi and Kyushu regions, transported by ship/pipeline to offshore saline aquifers west of Kyushu. FID target FY2026; operations by 2030. Coordinates approximate for planned offshore saline storage area W of Kyushu.
